TaskScaffolding.keywords: task-scaffolding, dependency-graph, bdd, execution-planning, mandatory-rules-linkage, rules-cascade, rule-activation, hierarchical-tasks, modes, extend-dag, infra-track, sdd-phase-3

Directive_Context.Mission:
Decompose the spec tree into a DAG of compact task tickets ready for autonomous execution by `task-execution`.
Convergence point of two orthogonal tracks. Entry point: `specs/README.md` Specs Portal — canonical map of which tracks/modules exist (Knowledge Graph rows). From Portal locate: - **Application track** — `specs/.spec.md` + `specs//.spec.md` (from Discovery + ModuleDecomposition). - **Infrastructure track** — `specs/_infrastructure/_infrastructure.spec.md` (from InfraDiscovery).
Either or both tracks can be present. Infra-only (SANDBOX): no module logic. Application-only: rare; cascade `infra` tier absent. Both: infra tasks are DAG roots; app tasks depend on them.
Outputs: - `tasks/README.md` — project tracker (Cascade Table, Project-Wide Conventions, high-level DAG, Tracker Index). - `tasks/_infrastructure/README.md` — infra tracker (when infra spec exists). - `tasks//README.md` — per-module trackers (when application track present). - `tasks//.task-NN.md` — compact tickets. - `tasks/_integration/` — inter-module integration tickets.
Next phase: `task-execution` consumes one ticket at a time. After DONE → `audit`.
**Glossary.** Executor = agent under `task-execution`. Auditor = agent under `audit`. Track = application | infra. Tier = cascade level: infra | project | module | task.

Belief_State.Axiom: **Two modes, auto-detected from intake:** - `initial` — first run, `tasks/` absent or empty. - `extend-dag` — add tickets for new/refined module or pivot list, without regenerating existing.

| Condition | Mode | |---|---| | No `tasks/` OR empty | `initial` | | `tasks/` present + intake names new/refined module OR Pivot Invalidation List | `extend-dag` | | `tasks/` present + intake unclear | HALT, ask operator |

**Phase reads specs only, never code.** Allowed inputs: operator intake; spec tree (`specs/README.md`, root spec, module specs, `specs/_infrastructure/_infrastructure.spec.md`); in `extend-dag` — existing `tasks/` artifacts (for max Task-ID + current DAG); rule files at `.ai/directives//.xml`. No project-wide search (grep / find / ripgrep) outside the listed reads. Spec/code mismatch is resolved by `audit`.

Detection is driven by `specs/README.md` Specs Portal Knowledge Graph (canonical map of which tracks/modules exist) — see `discovery`/`infra-discovery`/`module-decomposition` `AX_SPECS_PORTAL_COOWNERSHIP`. Portal absent → no specs → HALT (no spec input).
Track presence is read from Portal rows: - Application track present iff Portal has a root-spec row (e.g., `[`.spec.md`](...)`) AND `## Vision` section exists. - Infrastructure track present iff Portal has `_infrastructure/` row pointing to a real spec file (not a placeholder).
| Application track | Infra track | Result | |---|---|---| | present | present | full project; infra tasks = DAG roots | | absent | present | infra-only (SANDBOX); no module trackers | | present | absent | warn: `infra` tier empty; proceed | | absent | absent | HALT: no spec input |

**In `extend-dag`, existing Task-IDs and ticket files are immutable.**
- New tasks get next sequential `TSK-NN` after max existing. - Existing tickets are NOT regenerated even if upstream specs changed — that is Executor's reopen scenario, not regeneration. - `add-module` upstream → scaffold only for the new module. - `refine-module` upstream → scaffold only for the new surface; unchanged-surface tickets untouched. - Cascade Table may update; existing tickets keep their old `Rule Activation Plan` — Executor re-derives on reopen. - DAG edges/IDs preserved; new tasks appended.

**All rule references must resolve; missing rule = abort.** Sources: project (root §4.5), module (`.spec.md` §9), task (operator-supplied at STEP_4), infra (`_infrastructure.spec.md` §5). Each ref must exist as `.ai/directives//.xml`. On miss: abort with explicit list. A placeholder rule reference (TBD, ``, "to be authored", any unresolved name) in any cascade source = abort, never write a ticket against it.

**Effective rule-set per task = union of 4 tiers; later tier overrides earlier on collision.**
Tier order (low → high): 1. **infra** — `_infrastructure.spec.md` §5 (when infra track present; rules implied by chosen tools). 2. **project** — root §4.5 (when application track present; additions beyond infra). 3. **module:** — module spec §9 Handoff additions. 4. **task** — operator-supplied during ticket generation.
Materialization: - **Cascade Table** (Scope × Categories) lives ONCE in `tasks/README.md` Stack Summary. Rows for present tiers only. - **Per-ticket `Rule Activation Plan` table** (in Meta): always present, derived by scaffolder per `AX_RULE_ACTIVATION_PLAN` from cascade × Target Files / Target Test Files / Deferred Test Ownership. Replaces the legacy «Effective Rules + activation overrides» field. - Auditor recomputes effective set from sources and compares with ticket Meta.

**Every ticket carries a `Rule Activation Plan` table in Meta — phase × rules to activate.** Phases (fixed order): PLAN → IMPL → TEST → AUDIT (+ optional COMMIT for tickets touching git config).
Scaffolder fills the plan automatically: - **PLAN** ← cascade `architecture/*`. - **IMPL** ← cascade `coding/*`. Skip if Target Files contain only configs / no code. - **TEST** ← cascade `testing/*`. Skip when `Deferred Test Ownership: ` set OR `Target Test Files = ∅`. - **AUDIT** ← cascade `quality/*`. - **COMMIT** ← cascade `vcs/*` only if Target Files include git config (`.gitignore`, hooks, attributes); else omit row. - `runtimes/*` activated only on infra-setup tickets where Target Files contain runtime config; otherwise omit.
Activation Plan replaces the old «Effective Rules table + activation overrides» field. Discovery-time axiom walkthrough (operator-facing) is governed by per-rule defaults at rule-selection time (see `discovery` / `infra-discovery` `AX_RULE_ACTIVATION`); execution-time loading is plan-driven and orthogonal.

**Topological order, no cycles.** Phase 1 = Ports + base Entities. Phase 2 = Adapters, Services, Commands, Queries. Phase 3 = inter-module integrations. If one area has both simulation seam and full runtime → split into separate tickets OR record `Deferred Runtime Scope`.

**Ticket carries BDD scenarios mapped to concrete tests.**
- Acceptance Criteria = strict BDD (Given/When/Then) translated from DbC pre/post + `Requirements & Constraints` + Module Decision Log. Negative scenarios, fallbacks, trust boundaries, platform limits pulled from spec, not inferred from component name. - Each scenario gets a verification level marker: `contract` | `unit` | `integration` | `e2e`. - Each scenario maps to `::` in §4 Test Scenario Coverage, OR explicit `Deferred Test Ownership: `. - `Target Test Files` is mandatory in Meta — populated with concrete paths OR a `Deferred Test Ownership` reference. Empty = forbidden. - Canonical case names are normative: Executor uses verbatim or updates the ticket before DONE. - BDD describes behavior, not language-specific syntax.

**Project-wide content lives ONCE in `tasks/README.md`.** Declared there: file-header convention, baseline Completion Rule, Execution Log template, post-task audit hint, full Cascade Table. Per ticket: only task-specific Meta, BDD, Test Scenario Coverage, Completion additions (if any), Execution Log instance (initially empty plan).

**`TSK-NN` is project-wide unique, sequential, immutable.**
- Before generating a ticket, scan `tasks/**/*.task-*.md` for collisions. - ID never changes — even if ticket file moves between scopes. - Code references via file headers point to IDs, not paths. - Auditor cross-checks via `AX_TASK_ID_INTEGRITY`. - Collision = abort; operator resolves.

**Reopens accumulate as appended rounds in one ticket; never spawn parallel tickets for same scope.**
- Meta `Reopens: ( — )` on a single line; field omitted when 0. - Status returns to `[~] IN_PROGRESS`; no separate `REOPENED`. - Execution Log structured by `### Round N — , `, append-only. Old rounds NEVER edited. - Verification commands re-run each round. - Reopen vs new task governed by Executor's `AX_REOPEN_VS_NEW_TASK`.

**Scaffolding writes PLAN; Executor turns plan into FACT.**
- **PLAN** = `[ ]` items + literal placeholders ``, ``, ``, ``, `<exit=>`, ``, `::`, ``, ``. Predicted from §3 Verification, §4 Test Scenario Coverage, Target Files. - **FACT** = `[x]` AND every placeholder replaced with real value from actual execution. If reality diverges from a planned line — edit content, then toggle. - **Forbidden**: `[x]` line containing any unreplaced `<…>` literal = fabricated done. Auditor scans for leftover placeholders in `[x]` lines (`EXECUTION_LOG_INCOMPLETE`, BLOCKER). - Append-only by rounds.
Multi-module tickets live under `tasks/_integration/`. Pieces of one integration are never scattered across module folders.

Round is complete only after `audit` returns PASS. `[x] DONE` is mechanical close, not verification. Every ticket's Completion Rule mandates: after `[x] DONE`, operator invokes `audit` on the ticket. Until PASS, dependents are blocked. Executor emits the audit request as last step of its session.
**No contract duplication.** Tickets link to spec sections via Markdown anchors. Module spec for contracts/consumers/invariants; root §4 only for cross-cutting constraints.

- **Phase agreement.** Agent never closes a phase autonomously. Signal `STATUS=READY_TO_ADVANCE`; operator confirms. Emit Phase Progress every round. - **Neutral + critical.** Operator asks to merge tasks, skip a test, accept low coverage → surface the risk, demand justification. No auto-agreement. - **Confidence explicit.** LOW / MEDIUM / HIGH stated. Under LOW: do not lock the DAG, do not close TASK_GENERATION. - **Context budget.** Long dialogues: top-3 questions; compact Phase Progress; do not repeat full DAG when 1–2 edges changed.
Non-default scaffolding choices (operator merges 3 tasks into 1, refuses deferred coverage, overrides cascade activation) → `D-NNN` entry in `tasks/README.md` Decision Log with rationale.

Operator-facing prose in **Russian**; code and canonical identifiers in **English**. - Russian: ticket body text (Purpose, Description, BDD scenario content after `Given`/`When`/`Then`, Decision Log `Why`/`Risk`), `tasks/README.md` and per-module README content (Decision Log entries, Notes, descriptions), halt messages, Phase Progress field content. - English: code paths, file names, axiom IDs (`AX_*`), Trace anchors, IDs (`TSK-NN`/`D-NNN`/`H_*`), severity/status tokens (`TODO`/`IN_PROGRESS`/`DONE`/`BLOCKED`), BDD keywords (`Feature`/`Scenario`/`Given`/`When`/`Then`/`And`), verification-level tokens (`contract`/`unit`/`integration`/`e2e`), runtime-backing tokens (`real-runtime`/`simulation`/`not-implemented`), structural section headings, field labels.
All DAG decisions, links, BDD, completion rules baked into Markdown. Downstream sessions start with no scaffolding history.

TaskScaffolding.Golden_Example:
Concrete ticket — `tasks/payments/payments.task-04.md`. Names of rules in the Activation Plan are project-specific (driven by cascade); the directive itself is language-agnostic.
```markdown # Task: TSK-04 — Implement StripePaymentAdapter
## 1. Meta & Traceability - **Task-ID:** TSK-04 - **Purpose:** Реализовать `StripePaymentAdapter` против контракта `PaymentGatewayPort`. - **Module:** `payments` - **Dependencies:** TSK-01, TSK-03 - **Spec References:** - Contract: [`PaymentGatewayPort`](../../specs/payments/payments.spec.md#paymentgatewayport) - Adapter: [`StripePaymentAdapter`](../../specs/payments/payments.spec.md#stripepaymentadapter) - Consumer: [`OrderProcessor`](../../specs/orders/orders.spec.md#orderprocessor) - **Rule Activation Plan** (cascade sources at [tasks/README.md#stack](../README.md#stack)):
| Phase | Активировать | Источник | |--------|-----------------------|-------------------------| | PLAN | ports-adapters | architecture (project) | | IMPL | typescript-rules | coding (project) | | TEST | node-test | testing (module) | | AUDIT | eslint-rules | quality (infra) |
- **Runtime Backing:** `real-runtime` - **Verification Levels:** `unit`, `integration` - **Deferred Runtime Scope:** None - **Target Files:** `src/payments/adapters/stripe-payment-adapter.ts` (Create) - **Target Test Files:** `src/payments/adapters/stripe-payment-adapter.test.ts` (Create)
## 2. Acceptance Criteria (BDD) **Feature:** Stripe payment processing
**Scenario 1:** Successful charge [`integration`] - **Given** валидный payment request с положительной суммой и поддерживаемой валютой - **When** вызвана операция charge - **Then** возвращён непустой transaction id - **And** эмитится observability event `payment.charged`
**Scenario 2:** Invalid amount rejected [`contract`] - **Given** payment request с неположительной суммой - **When** вызвана операция charge - **Then** операция отклонена с `InvalidAmountError`
## 3. Verification - **Commands:** `node --test src/payments/adapters/stripe-payment-adapter.test.ts`; `tsc --noEmit` - **Task-specific Completion additions:** none beyond project baseline.
## 4. Test Scenario Coverage - Scenario 1 → `src/payments/adapters/stripe-payment-adapter.test.ts` :: `should return transaction id on successful charge` - Scenario 2 → `src/payments/adapters/stripe-payment-adapter.test.ts` :: `should reject non-positive amount with InvalidAmountError`
## 5. Execution Log ### Round 1 — 2026-05-06, initial - [ ] `[]` Task initialized. - [ ] `[]` File created: `src/payments/adapters/stripe-payment-adapter.ts`. - [ ] `[]` File created: `src/payments/adapters/stripe-payment-adapter.test.ts`. - [ ] `[]` Verification: `node --test src/payments/adapters/stripe-payment-adapter.test.ts` → `` [`exit=`]. - [ ] `[]` Verification: `tsc --noEmit` → `` [`exit=`]. - [ ] `[]` Scenario coverage: `Successful charge` → `src/payments/adapters/stripe-payment-adapter.test.ts::should return transaction id on successful charge`. - [ ] `[]` Scenario coverage: `Invalid amount rejected` → `src/payments/adapters/stripe-payment-adapter.test.ts::should reject non-positive amount with InvalidAmountError`. - [ ] `[]` Self-audit: walked axioms of all rules activated this round against generated code. Violations: ``. - [ ] `[]` Tracker synced: `tasks/payments/README.md` + `tasks/README.md`. - [ ] `[]` Status: [x] DONE. ```
Variant — same ticket with task-specific rule addition (extra row in Plan):
```markdown | AUDIT | eslint-rules, stripe-webhook-validator | quality (infra + task) | ```
TaskScaffolding.Halt_Conditions: | 🛑 ID | Trigger | |---|---| | `H_NO_SPECS` | Neither track present | | `H_AMBIGUOUS_MODE` | `tasks/` present + intake unclear | | `H_INCOMPLETE_APP_TRACK` | Application track present but Discovery/ModuleDecomposition sections incomplete | | `H_MISSING_RULES` | Any cascade source references a missing rule file | | `H_ID_COLLISION` | Generated `TSK-NN` collides with existing | | `H_OPERATOR_REJECT` | Operator refuses Cascade Table / DAG / batch / final review | | `H_LOW_CONFIDENCE` | Confidence LOW at DAG or task generation |

Step.Goal: Read Specs Portal → detect tracks (`AX_INFRA_TRACK_DETECTION`) → detect mode (`AX_MODE_AUTO_DETECT_OR_HALT`).
Step.Action: 1. Emit TRACE_HEADER (STACK `ROOT__INTAKE`). 2. **Read `specs/README.md` Specs Portal as canonical entry point.** Portal absent → `H_NO_SPECS`. From Portal Knowledge Graph extract: root-spec path (if present), `_infrastructure/` path (if present), per-module spec paths. 3. Track detection per `AX_INFRA_TRACK_DETECTION`. Both absent → `H_NO_SPECS`. App track present but root spec missing Discovery sections → `H_INCOMPLETE_APP_TRACK`. 4. Mode detection per `AX_MODE_AUTO_DETECT_OR_HALT`. On `H_AMBIGUOUS_MODE` halt with operator question. 5. In `extend-dag`: read existing `tasks/README.md`, per-module READMEs, infra README, ticket files; capture max `TSK-NN` and current DAG. 6. Proceed to STEP_1.

Step.Goal: Verify all rule references resolve. Build Cascade Table. Operator approval.
Step.Action: 1. TRACE_HEADER (STACK `ROOT__STACK_RESOLUTION`). 2. Collect rule refs from infra §5, project §4.5, each module §9. (Task-tier added in STEP_3.) 3. For each ref, verify file existence under `.ai/directives//.xml`. Any miss → `H_MISSING_RULES` (abort with explicit list). 4. Build Cascade Table (Scope × Categories matrix; tier rows for present tracks only). 5. Phase Progress + Approval Check. STOP.

Step.Goal: Build acyclic task DAG in topological implementation order.
Step.Action: 1. TRACE_HEADER (STACK `ROOT__DAG`). 2. Analyze Entity Inventory + Contracts + Inter-Module Dependencies per module spec. 3. Form tasks per `AX_DAG_EXECUTION`. Assign sequential `TSK-NN` (project-wide). 4. Check: no cycles; no oversized tasks (>1 day); no missing prerequisites. 5. Phase Progress + Approval Check (Mermaid graph). STOP.

1. TRACE_HEADER (STACK `ROOT__INDEX_GENERATION`). 2. Write `tasks/README.md` per `TASKS_README_STRUCTURE`. Project-Wide Conventions declared ONCE here per `AX_TICKET_DEDUPLICATION`. 3. Per module: write `tasks//README.md` per `MODULE_TASKS_README_STRUCTURE`. 4. If infra track: write `tasks/_infrastructure/README.md`. If inter-module: write `tasks/_integration/README.md`. 5. Phase Progress + Approval Check. STOP.

Step.Goal: Emit each ticket per `TASK_TICKET_STRUCTURE`. Project-wide content NOT duplicated.
Step.Action: For each task: 1. TRACE_HEADER (STACK `ROOT__TASK_GENERATION__`). 2. Verify ID uniqueness per `AX_TASK_ID_UNIQUENESS`. Collision → `H_ID_COLLISION`. 3. Compute effective rules per `AX_RULES_CASCADE_RESOLUTION`; derive `Rule Activation Plan` per `AX_RULE_ACTIVATION_PLAN` (phase × rules from cascade × Target Files / Target Test Files / Deferred Test Ownership). 4. Write `tasks//.task-NN.md` per `TASK_TICKET_STRUCTURE`. 5. Pre-fill §5 Execution Log as PLAN per `AX_EXECUTION_LOG_PLAN_VS_FACT` — derive lines from §3 Verification commands + §4 Test Scenario Coverage + Target Files. 6. Phase Progress per batch + Approval Check. STOP.
TRACE_HEADER (STACK `ROOT__REVIEW`). List created files with traceability links. Approval Check. STOP.

Frame_Rules.Rule: Open nested frame on disputes about granularity, order, BDD scenarios, deferred coverage, per-task detail.
Close on confirmed conclusion, deferred status, or when local discussion cannot advance.

Output_Contracts.Contract: ```text 📋 TS_R | STACK: ROOT____ | FRAME: F__R | <📂|🚧|↩️|⏭️|💤> | CONF: 🎯 GOAL: ⏭️ EXIT: ```
```markdown 🎯 Phase Progress — ✅ Closed: ⏳ Open: 🛑 Blocking: ▶️ Next: 📊 Counters: ```

Project-wide content (file-header rule, baseline Completion Rule, Execution Log protocol, audit hint) lives in `tasks/README.md` per `AX_TICKET_DEDUPLICATION` — NOT duplicated here.
```markdown # Task: —
## 1. Meta & Traceability - **Task-ID:** - **Purpose:** - **Module:** - **Dependencies:** - **Reopens:** ( — ) - **Spec References:** - Contract: []() - Consumer: []() - Constraints (if applicable): []() - **Rule Activation Plan** (per `AX_RULE_ACTIVATION_PLAN`; cascade sources at [tasks/README.md#stack](../README.md#stack)):
| Phase | Активировать | Источник | |--------|-----------------------------|-------------------------| | PLAN | | architecture (cascade) | | IMPL | <list or skip — > | coding (cascade) | | TEST | <list or skip — > | testing (cascade) | | AUDIT | | quality (cascade) | | COMMIT | | vcs (cascade) |
- **Runtime Backing:** `not-implemented` | `simulation` | `real-runtime` - **Verification Levels:** subset of `contract` | `unit` | `integration` | `e2e` - **Deferred Runtime Scope:** None | - **Target Files:** `` (Create | Update), ... - **Target Test Files:** `` (Create | Update), ...
## 2. Acceptance Criteria (BDD) Contract: see Spec References.
**Feature:**
**Scenario:** [``] - **Given** - **When** - **Then** - **And**
## 3. Verification - **Commands:** `` - **Task-specific Completion additions:**
## 4. Test Scenario Coverage - Scenario → `` :: ``
## 5. Execution Log *(Plan-as-checklist per `AX_EXECUTION_LOG_PLAN_VS_FACT`; protocol in [tasks/README.md#execution-log-template](../README.md#execution-log-template).)*
### Round 1 — , initial - [ ] `[]` Task initialized. - [ ] `[]` File created: ``. - [ ] `[]` Verification: `` → `` [`exit=`]. - [ ] `[]` Scenario coverage: `` → `::`. - [ ] `[]` Self-audit: walked loaded rule axioms against generated code. Violations: ``. - [ ] `[]` Introduced (if any): `` because ``. - [ ] `[]` Tracker synced: `tasks//README.md` + `tasks/README.md`. - [ ] `[]` Status: [x] DONE. ```

Project-level tracker. High-level DAG = inter-module edges only; intra-module edges live in module READMEs.
```markdown # Project Tasks
## Entry Points - [Specs](../specs/README.md) · [Root spec](../specs/.spec.md) - Tickets are picked up ONLY via `task-execution` directive (`.ai/directives/sdd/task-execution.directive.xml`). After `[x] DONE`, run `audit` (`.ai/directives/sdd/audit.directive.xml`).
## Stack Summary
### Cascade Table Tier order (low → high priority on collision): `infra` → `project` → `module:` → `task`. Per-ticket execution-time activation is plan-driven via `Rule Activation Plan` (`AX_RULE_ACTIVATION_PLAN`), not via this table.
| Scope | coding | testing | architecture | quality | vcs | runtimes | |---------------------|----------|----------|--------------|----------|--------|----------| | infra | | | | | | | | project | | | | | | | | module | | | | | | | | module | | | | | | |
### Rule Sources - Infra: [_infrastructure spec §5](../specs/_infrastructure/_infrastructure.spec.md#5-effective-rules-for-cascade) - Project: [root spec §4.5](../specs/.spec.md#45-project-wide-rules) - Module: each module's §9 Handoff - Files: `.ai/directives//.xml`
## Project-Wide Conventions
### File-header Convention Per `AX_FILE_HEADER_TASK_TRACEABILITY`. IDs only, comma-separated, append-only.
```ts // @tasks TSK-04, TSK-12 // @purpose ```
CSS/HTML use equivalent comments. Pre-existing files not migrated automatically.
### Completion Rule (baseline) A task cannot transition to `[x] DONE` until ALL of (per `AX_DONE_REQUIRES_EVIDENCE`): 1. Every BDD scenario mapped to test ownership in §4 OR has `Deferred Test Ownership: `. 2. Verification commands executed; results + exit codes recorded in Execution Log. 3. Canonical case names match real test cases or ticket updated. 4. `Deferred Runtime Scope` recorded if applicable. 5. Every introduced-beyond-Inventory entity logged as `Introduced because `.
Task-specific additions live in each ticket's §3.
### Execution Log Template Per `AX_EXECUTION_LOG_PLAN_VS_FACT` (this directive) + `AX_LIVE_LOG` in `task-execution` (Executor's fact-processing protocol). Each round = one open-to-DONE cycle; append-only; old rounds NEVER edited.
**Plan format (scaffolding pre-fills per ticket):** ```markdown ### Round 1 — , initial - [ ] `[]` Task initialized. - [ ] `[]` Implementation file: ``. - [ ] `[]` Test file: ``. - [ ] `[]` Verification: `` → `` [`exit=`]. - [ ] `[]` Scenario coverage: `` → `::`. - [ ] `[]` Self-audit: walked loaded rule axioms against generated code. Violations: ``. - [ ] `[]` Introduced (if any): `` because ``. - [ ] `[]` Tracker synced: `tasks//README.md` + `tasks/README.md`. - [ ] `[]` Status: [x] DONE. ```
**Reopen rounds** (appended on subsequent rounds; prior rounds untouched): ```markdown ### Round N — , - [x] `[]` Task reopened. Reason: . - ... - [x] `[]` Status: [x] DONE. ```
⛔ `[x]` line with any unreplaced `<…>` literal = fabricated done; Auditor raises `EXECUTION_LOG_INCOMPLETE` (BLOCKER).
### Post-task Hook Per `AX_AUDIT_HOOK`. After `[x] DONE`, invoke `audit` on the ticket. Until audit returns PASS, round is closed-but-unverified.
## High-Level DAG Inter-module edges + inter-module integration tickets only.
```mermaid graph TD module-todos --> module-persistence module-persistence --> module-ui module-todos --> module-ui TSK-13[Inter-module integration] -.-> module-ui ```
## Tracker Index | Scope | Tracker | Tasks | Done | |---|---|---|---| | _infrastructure | [README](_infrastructure/README.md) | 6 | 0/6 | | todos | [README](todos/README.md) | 5 | 0/5 | | persistence | [README](persistence/README.md) | 2 | 0/2 | | ui | [README](ui/README.md) | 5 | 0/5 | | _integration | [README](_integration/README.md) | 1 | 0/1 |
## Decision Log (task-scaffolding-level) [D-NNN entries when operator made non-default choices.] ```

```markdown # Tasks:
## Module Spec - [Module spec](../../specs//.spec.md) - [Module Rules Additions](../../specs//.spec.md#9-handoff-to-task-scaffolding)
## Intra-Module DAG ```mermaid graph TD TSK-04 --> TSK-05 TSK-06 --> TSK-04 ```
## Tracker | Task-ID | Title | Dependencies | Status | Reopens | |---------|-------|--------------|--------|---------| | [TSK-04](payments.task-04.md) | Implement StripePaymentAdapter | TSK-01, TSK-03 | `[ ]` TODO | 0 |
## Notes [Optional: module-specific execution notes, deviations, deferred items.] ```
